label: Vayikra (Leviticus) — Part 2 — Chapters 17–27
summary_plain: Vayikra Part 2 contains the Holiness Code. YHWH commands: You shall be holy, for I YHWH your Creator am holy. The laws cover forbidden practices, how to treat one another, and the commandment: you shall love your neighbor as yourself — I am YHWH. YHWH gives the Mo'adim — the appointed times: the Pesach, the Feast of Unleavened Bread, the day of first fruits, the counting of seven weeks, the first day of the seventh month (a day of solemn rest and trumpet blast), Yom Kippur (the tenth day of the seventh month), and the Feast of Booths. YHWH gives the Shemitah — every seventh year the land rests. And the Jubilee — the fiftieth year, when liberty is proclaimed throughout the land and every person returns to their property. The book ends with the covenant blessings and curses: if you walk in my statutes I will walk among you and be your Creator, and you shall be my people.
summary_scholarly: Chapters 17 through 27 contain the Holiness Code, the Mo'adim calendar, the Shemitah and Jubilee laws, and the covenant blessings and curses. The ACR reads from the Dead Sea Scrolls (4QLev-a through 4QLev-f) and the Orit Ge'ez of the African Israelite community. The Masoretic is noted as a variant only. Chapter 19 is the center of the Holiness Code: 'You shall be holy, for I YHWH your Creator am holy' (19:2), culminating in 'you shall love your neighbor as yourself — I am YHWH' (19:18). The Mo'adim in chapter 23 are not 'Jewish holidays' — they are YHWH's appointed times, announced as 'my appointed feasts' (23:2). The Jubilee in chapter 25 is the fiftieth year: 'proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants' (25:10). The Yovelim text (ACR Volume 7) takes its name from this institution and structures all of history by jubilee cycles. The covenant terms in chapter 26 are direct: 'I will walk among you and will be your Creator, and you shall be my people' (26:12).

key_terms.definition: Chapters 17-26 of Vayikra. The central command is 'You shall be holy, for I YHWH your Creator am holy' (19:2). Every law in these chapters flows from that principle.
'You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself — I am YHWH' (19:18).

The appointed times of YHWH (ch 23). YHWH says: 'These are my appointed feasts' (23:2). They include: Pesach, Unleavened Bread, First Fruits, Shavuot (Weeks), the first of the seventh month, Yom Kippur, and Sukkot (Booths).
The seventh-year rest for the land (25:1-7). 'In the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the land, a Sabbath to YHWH.'

The fiftieth year: 'Proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you, when each of you shall return to his property' (25:10). The Yovelim text (ACR Volume 7) is named after this institution.

The Feast of Booths — seven days beginning the fifteenth of the seventh month (23:34). The people dwell in booths to remember that YHWH made the children of Yisra'EL dwell in booths when he brought them out of Egypt.
'In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at twilight, is YHWH's Pesach' (23:5). Listed first among the appointed times.
Chapter 26: if you walk in YHWH's statutes, the rains come in season, the land yields, enemies flee. 'I will walk among you and will be your Creator, and you shall be my people' (26:12).
Chapter 26: if you reject YHWH's statutes, the consequences are severe — disease, defeat, exile. But even then, YHWH says he will not utterly destroy them or break his covenant (26:44).
The phrase that closes nearly every law in the Holiness Code. After each commandment — 'I am YHWH' or 'I am YHWH your Creator.' The laws are grounded in who is speaking them.

fill_blank.prompt: You shall be ______, for I YHWH your Creator am ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: Speak to all the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: You shall be holy, for I YHWH your Creator am holy.

fill_blank.prompt: You shall love your ______ as yourself — I am YHWH.
fill_blank.source_quote: You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself — I am YHWH.

fill_blank.prompt: These are the appointed feasts of YHWH that you shall proclaim as holy ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: Speak to the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: These are the appointed feasts of YHWH that you shall proclaim as holy convocations — they are my appointed feasts.

fill_blank.prompt: And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year, and proclaim ______ throughout the land to all its inhabitants.
fill_blank.source_quote: And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you, when each of you shall return to his property.

fill_blank.prompt: And I will walk among you and will be your Creator, and you shall be my ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: And I will walk among you and will be your Creator, and you shall be my people.

fill_blank.prompt: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at twilight, is YHWH's ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at twilight, is YHWH's Pesach.

fill_blank.prompt: But in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the ______, a Sabbath to YHWH.
fill_blank.source_quote: But in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the land, a Sabbath to YHWH. You shall not sow your field or prune your vineyard.

fill_blank.prompt: On the fifteenth day of this seventh month and for seven days is the Feast of ______ to YHWH.
fill_blank.source_quote: Speak to the people of Yisra'EL, saying: On the fifteenth day of this seventh month and for seven days is the Feast of Booths to YHWH.

fill_blank.prompt: You shall follow my rules and keep my statutes and walk in them. I am YHWH your ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: You shall follow my rules and keep my statutes and walk in them. I am YHWH your Creator.

fill_blank.prompt: Now on the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of ______.
fill_blank.source_quote: Now on the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. It shall be for you a time of holy convocation, and you shall afflict yourselves.

multiple_choice.question: What is the central command of the Holiness Code?
options: You shall be holy, for I YHWH your Creator am holy
You shall offer a burnt offering daily
multiple_choice.source_quote: Speak to all the congregation of the people of Yisra'EL and say to them: You shall be holy, for I YHWH your Creator am holy.

multiple_choice.question: What does YHWH command about your neighbor?
multiple_choice.source_quote: You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself — I am YHWH.

multiple_choice.question: What happens in the Jubilee (fiftieth) year?
options: Liberty is proclaimed and each person returns to their property
multiple_choice.source_quote: And you shall consecrate the fiftieth year, and proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants.

multiple_choice.question: Whose appointed feasts are the Mo'adim?
multiple_choice.source_quote: These are the appointed feasts of YHWH that you shall proclaim as holy convocations — they are my appointed feasts.

multiple_choice.question: What must happen every seventh year?
options: The land must rest — no sowing or pruning
A burnt offering for the nation
multiple_choice.source_quote: But in the seventh year there shall be a Sabbath of solemn rest for the land, a Sabbath to YHWH.

multiple_choice.question: What does YHWH promise if the people walk in his statutes?
options: He will walk among them and be their Creator, and they will be his people
He will make them kings over all nations
He will never speak to them again
multiple_choice.source_quote: And I will walk among you and will be your Creator, and you shall be my people.

multiple_choice.question: When is YHWH's Pesach?
options: The first day of the first month
The fourteenth day of the first month at twilight
The tenth day of the seventh month
The fifteenth day of the seventh month
multiple_choice.source_quote: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month at twilight, is YHWH's Pesach.

multiple_choice.question: How long is the Feast of Booths?
multiple_choice.source_quote: On the fifteenth day of this seventh month and for seven days is the Feast of Booths to YHWH.

multiple_choice.question: On which day of the seventh month is the Day of Atonement?
multiple_choice.source_quote: Now on the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement.

multiple_choice.question: What does YHWH tell Yisra'EL about the practices of Egypt and Kena'an?
options: You shall not do as they do — follow my rules instead
multiple_choice.source_quote: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived, and you shall not do as they do in the land of Kena'an, to which I am bringing you. You shall not walk in their statutes.

faq.question: What are the Mo'adim?
faq.answer: The appointed times of YHWH, listed in chapter 23. YHWH calls them 'my appointed feasts' (23:2). They are: the Sabbath, Pesach (14th of the first month), Unleavened Bread (15th-21st), First Fruits, Shavuot (counting seven weeks), the first day of the seventh month (trumpet blast), Yom Kippur (10th of the seventh month), and Sukkot / Feast of Booths (15th-21st of the seventh month). These are YHWH's calendar, not cultural custom.

faq.question: What is the Jubilee?
faq.answer: Every fiftieth year: 'proclaim liberty throughout the land to all its inhabitants' (25:10). Land returns to its original family. Debts are released. People who sold themselves into servitude go free. The entire economic order resets. The Yovelim text (ACR Volume 7) structures all of history by jubilee cycles — 49-year periods — and takes its name from this institution.

faq.question: What does 'love your neighbor as yourself' mean in context?
faq.answer: It appears in Vayikra 19:18 as part of the Holiness Code, immediately after: 'You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against the sons of your own people.' It is a direct command from YHWH, ending with 'I am YHWH' — meaning the authority behind the command is YHWH himself. It is not a suggestion or a proverb.

faq.question: Why does the phrase 'I am YHWH' appear after so many commandments?
faq.answer: The Holiness Code repeats 'I am YHWH' or 'I am YHWH your Creator' after nearly every law — after commands about justice, about treating the stranger, about the Mo'adim, about sexual conduct, about economic fairness. The repetition anchors every instruction in the identity of the one giving it. You obey because of who is speaking.

faq.question: What are the covenant blessings and curses in chapter 26?
faq.answer: If Yisra'EL walks in YHWH's statutes: rain in season, abundant harvest, peace, no fear, victory. 'I will walk among you and will be your Creator, and you shall be my people' (26:12). If they reject his statutes: disease, defeat, scattered among the nations. But even in exile, YHWH says: 'I will not utterly destroy them or break my covenant with them, for I am YHWH their Creator' (26:44).

faq.question: What is the Shemitah?
faq.answer: Every seventh year, the land itself keeps a Sabbath to YHWH (25:2-4). No sowing, no pruning, no harvesting what grows of itself commercially. The land rests. What grows naturally may be eaten, but not stored or sold. The seventh-year rest mirrors the seven-day Sabbath — the land is treated as YHWH's, not as property to be worked without limit.

faq.question: Why does the ACR call these 'YHWH's appointed times' instead of 'Jewish holidays'?
faq.answer: YHWH says: 'These are MY appointed feasts' (23:2). The Mo'adim are not Jewish holidays — they are YHWH's calendar, set in the heavenly tablets (Yovelim 6), confirmed in the 364-day solar calendar at Qumran (4Q320, 4Q321), and preserved by the Beta Israel of Ethiopia in pre-Rabbinical form. The Rabbinical calendar was standardized by Hillel II approximately 359 CE using the Babylonian Metonic cycle with Babylonian month names (Nisan, Tammuz, Elul). The Qumran community separated from Jerusalem precisely because the Temple authorities adopted this Babylonian lunar system.

faq.question: What does the Jubilee framework reveal about the covenant economy?
faq.answer: The Jubilee in Vayikra 25 — proclaim liberty throughout the land, each person returns to their property — is the most radical economic framework in any ancient text. Every 50th year: land returns to its original family, debts are released, people in servitude go free. The Yovelim text (ACR Volume 7) structures all of history by jubilee cycles. The phrase on the American Liberty Bell — 'proclaim liberty throughout the land' — comes from Vayikra 25:10. The nation that inscribed this covenant text on its bell held millions of covenant people in chattel slavery while it was ringing.
